UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the EcoPulse application, separate from authentication credentials managed by an external system. This entity stores public or administrative user data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user in the external authentication system. This links the local profile to the authentication provider's user record. (Relationship: ExternalAuthSystem 1:1 UserProfile)

username.description: A display name or chosen username for the user within the EcoPulse platform.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily used for communication and identification purposes, not for direct authentication by EcoPulse.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (e.g., 'policymaker', 'researcher'). This can determine access levels or dashboard views.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this user profile was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID (`userId`) directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID, ensuring path-based ownership. The 'id' and 'externalAuthId' fields within the document will also store this 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns this profile.

definition.description: Centralized storage for diverse real-time climate observations fetched from external APIs. This collection is designed for authenticated users to have read-only access. Write access is restricted to backend services or specific admin roles, ensuring a homogeneous security posture. QAPs are supported as all authenticated users have the same read permissions.

params.name: climateDataId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific climate data point or record.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated insights saved by individual users. Each insight document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching '{userId}') for robust authorization independence, eliminating the need for 'get()' operations in rules. QAPs are supported as list operations implicitly filter by the current user's UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns these insights.

params.name: insightId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific saved AI-generated insight.

definition.description: Stores custom reports generated and saved by individual users. Each report document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching '{userId}') for robust authorization independence, eliminating the need for 'get()' operations in rules. QAPs are supported as list operations implicitly filter by the current user's UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns these reports.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific saved report.

definition.description: This collection implements the "existence over content" pattern for global administrative roles. The existence of a document at 'roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}' grants administrator privileges. This provides a clear and efficient way to manage global access, leveraging direct path validation for rules.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability following the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence:**
*   For user-specific data like `/userProfiles/{userId}`, `/users/{userId}/savedInsights/{insightId}`, and `/users/{userId}/savedReports/{reportId}`, ownership is enforced directly through the path (`userId == request.auth.uid`). Additionally, for subcollections like `savedInsights` and `savedReports`, each document explicitly includes a denormalized `ownerId` field (mirroring the `userId` from the path). This critical denormalization eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check parent ownership, ensuring atomic write operations, simplifying rule logic, and enhancing debuggability. 
*   The `/climateData/{climateDataId}` collection, intended for global read-only access by authenticated users, inherently requires no `get()` calls for authorization as its access policy is uniform across all documents. 
*   The `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection uses the 'existence over content' pattern for global roles. While this pattern involves a `get()` call within security rules (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`), it is a direct, efficient, and idiomatic check for global role assignment, not a hierarchical authorization dependency that would break atomicity.

**Querying for Authorized Paths (QAPs - Rules are not Filters):**
*   **`/userProfiles/{userId}`:** Access rules will restrict reads and writes to `request.auth.uid == userId`. This inherently secures `list` operations, as users can only query their own profile path. 
*   **`/users/{userId}/savedInsights/{insightId}` & `/users/{userId}/savedReports/{reportId}`:** These subcollections are organized under a user's specific path. Security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path AND the `ownerId` field within the document. This dual verification ensures that `list` operations within these paths are inherently secure and implicitly filtered by the authenticated user's ID. A user cannot query another user's insights or reports. 
*   **`/climateData/{climateDataId}`:** Since all authenticated users have uniform read access to this collection, `list` operations are inherently secure. The rules will simply allow reads for any authenticated user, ensuring no unintended data exposure. 
*   **`/roles_admin/{adminId}`:** Users can only attempt to access their own admin role document, securing list operations against unauthorized enumeration of administrators.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** `userProfiles` and the `users/{userId}/...` subcollections use path-based ownership, ensuring data privacy for individual users. 
*   **Homogeneous Security:** Each collection (e.g., `userProfiles`, `climateData`) and subcollection (e.g., `savedInsights`) maintains a consistent security posture, simplifying rule sets significantly. For instance, all documents in `climateData` are globally readable by authenticated users, and all documents in `/users/{userId}/savedInsights` are owned by `userId`. 
*   **Global Roles:** The `roles_admin` collection uses an 'existence over content' model for efficient, top-level role checks, separate from per-profile roles.
